text: Fairy Fox Games has its own little emblem now — the game-farm icon (a sprout growing from a game controller). It sits beside the title on the home page, it's the icon in your browser tab, and when you share a link the icon shows as the preview image.

Arc no longer drops target pads at random distances one after another — each run now walks a shifting sequence of named range formations, so no two plays range the same. You'll ease through a calm Drift, walk a Ladder of pads stepping out (or back) a rung at a time, whip your power near-then-far across a Bracket, settle into a Groove that dares you to dial one distance in and repeat it, press to the far edge on a Reach, and weather a dense Fusillade crescendo. Climbing the stages opens the pool: early runs stay gentle, and the demanding formations unlock and take over as you get deeper. The notable ones flash their name as they arrive.

Orbit Slingshot runs no longer scatter targets one at a time — each run now threads a shifting sequence of named target patterns, so no two plays trace the same path. You'll sweep a loose Belt, chase a bunched Cluster, chase a marching Ring around the planet, climb an outward Ladder, brave a planet-hugging Perihelion for close-pass bonuses, and get buried in a dense Swarm. Climbing the stages opens the pool: early runs stay gentle, and the daring patterns unlock and take over as you get deeper. The notable ones flash their name as they arrive.

Symmetry runs are now built from shifting cadences, so no two plays feel the same. Instead of orbs falling to one flat rule, each run strings together named patterns — a calm Mirror, a rewarding Reflection of twins, a rhythmic Cascade, a swinging Weave, a snap-decision Split, and a dense Kaleidoscope crescendo. Climbing the stages opens the pool: early runs stay gentle, and the meaner patterns unlock and take over as you get deeper. The notable ones flash their name as they arrive.
Filter the games right on the home page — tap a mechanic (Reflex, Physics, Timing…) above the grid to show just the games with that feel, or “All” to see everything again.

Browse by mechanic: a new page groups every game by how it plays — reflex, timing, physics, aim, balance, and more — so it's easy to find another game with the feel you liked. It's linked right under the games on the home page.
Getting into the games is smoother now — no more quick white flash when the page loads. Pages announce their background instantly, so you move from the hub into Fairy Fox Games with no flicker.

A brand-new game: sort the falling sparks. Each coloured spark drops down the middle — send it into the channel that matches its colour before it lands. The twist: the channels keep rearranging, so you have to read the row, not memorise a key. Route early for a rising combo, wait to be safe when it scrambles, and don't let three slip past. It speeds up and adds channels through the stages, and every run is built from shifting patterns — Shuffle, Cascade, Rush, The Churn.

No two blooms grow the same anymore: motes now arrive in shifting patterns — a sweeping Vine you chase across the field, a wide Ring you loop, a tight Thicket to thread your growing trail through, and a rare prism Spectrum greed-rush. The meaner patterns unlock as you climb the stages, so runs keep opening up the deeper you go.

New reading menu: tap the “Aa” button in the header to switch theme (light, sepia, or dark), pick an accent colour, and set the text size — the site remembers your choice, and it carries across the whole of fairyfox.io. The pages now match the main site's look, seamlessly.

Fairy Fox Games is now an AI-managed game farm: new games are planted (a fresh one every few days) and the ones already here keep growing deeper, on their own, over time. Same games you love — a clearer promise.

Every run is now a shifting sequence of target cadences — Even, Pulse, Near, Far, Climb, Scatter — and climbing the stages unlocks the trickier ones, so the rhythm of the catches keeps building and no two runs feel the same.

Every run is now built from a shifting sequence of named formations — a rhythmic Staircase, a restful Hold, a relentless Zipper, tight Bursts, and The Wall — pulled from a seeded pool, so no two runs share the same shape. The tougher ones announce themselves as you enter them.

New game: Arc. Hold to build power, release to lob a shot at a fixed 45° — the whole skill is judging the distance and dialling the power to land on the pad. Nail the bright centre for a bullseye and keep the combo alive across three lives.
Loft now nudges you when a run ends just short of your best — the honest “so close, one more” line.
New game: Symmetry. One control moves two catchers locked in a mirror; you often can't save both sides at once, so read ahead — and chase the gold twins for a bonus.
New game: Poise. Tilt a beam to balance a rolling ball and roll it over the glowing target without letting it slip off either end. It gets twitchier the longer you last.

Every game gained a run arc: named stages you climb, an ambient colour that shifts as you go, and lifetime badges that stick across runs — so no run is wasted and the collection keeps opening up the more you play.
Prism motes are now a real greed call — triple points, but they grow your trail three times as fast, so every one you eat costs you room.
The multiplier rework: land a needed flip at the last instant to grow a ×2…×9 combo; flip early to play safe and it resets to ×1. Ride the edge for a huge score, or take the steady trickle.
The collection now sorts by most-recently-updated, with each card showing when it last changed — an at-a-glance sense of what's fresh.
New game: Loft. Keep the glowing orbs aloft — tap a falling orb to bat it back up. You can only strike on the way down, and every few points another orb joins the air.
